purpose: CMake preset file (version 6, cmakeMinimumRequired 4.2.0). Two configure presets: 'vs2026-x64' (Visual Studio 18 2026 generator, used by local devs via tools/build-native.ps1) and 'vs2022-x64' (Visual Studio 17 2022 generator, used by the GitHub Actions release workflow since VS2026 preview is not on hosted runners). Each has matching Debug/Release/Package-Release buildPresets that drive cmake builds and the markdownplusplus_package custom target.
called_by: tools/build-native.ps1 (local dev — invokes `cmake --preset vs2026-x64` and `cmake --build --preset vs2026-{debug,release}`)
.github/workflows/release.yml (CI — invokes `cmake --preset vs2022-x64` and `cmake --build --preset vs2022-release` on tag push)
invariants: Generator strings 'Visual Studio 18 2026' and 'Visual Studio 17 2022' must match the installed VS version's CMake generator name on each environment.

binaryDir and install prefix paths are referenced downstream — build/cmake/<preset>/package/<Config>/MarkdownPlusPlus. Changing them requires updating tools/install-native-plugin.ps1 and the release workflow.
Both presets must remain functionally equivalent (same MARKDOWNPP_BUILD_NATIVE=ON, same x64 arch) so binaries built by either are interchangeable.
gotchas: cmakeMinimumRequired here is 4.2.0 (newer than top-level CMakeLists's 3.24) because CMake 4 dropped support for compatibility floors below 3.5 - install-native-deps.py patches cmark-gfm's CMakeLists to raise its floor to 3.10 to avoid the warning.

The vs2022 preset exists for portability and CI builds — local devs should keep using vs2026 to stay aligned with build-native.ps1. If the local install scripts ever start consuming the vs2022 binaryDir, install-native-plugin.ps1's hard-coded vs2026-x64 path needs the matching change.
